title: Counting Primes Really Fast
snippet: Suppose you are given a very large number, for example $n = 10^{12}$, and you wish to know how many numbers equal to or less than $n$ are prime. This is the prime counting function, normally denotes as $\pi(n)$. In this article we will explore some of the methods to efficiently calculate $\pi(n)$...
